name: Missouri Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Missouri - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

address.description:
Missouri Board of Healing Arts
3605 Missouri Boulevard
P.O. Box 4
Jefferson City, MO 65102

renewalCycle: Every 2 years (by April 1, even years) — no CE required

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Card
requiredDocuments.description: Copy of your current, active Commission on Dietetic Registration (RD or RDN) credential.

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent from the degree-granting institution showing completion of an ACEND-accredited program.

requiredDocuments.name: Supervised Practice Verification
requiredDocuments.description: Documentation of completed ACEND-accredited dietetic internship hours.

requiredDocuments.name: Background Check Authorization
requiredDocuments.description: Completed authorization for the state background screening process.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Missouri dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application fee is $50. The biennial renewal fee is only $20, due by April 1 of even-numbered years. A $50 late fee applies if renewal is submitted after the 29-day grace period.

mainEntity.name: Does Missouri require continuing education for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. The State Committee of Dietitians does not mandate continuing education for state license renewal. However, you must maintain your national CDR registration, which requires 75 CPE units every five years.

mainEntity.name: What is the minimum age to apply for a Missouri dietitian license?

acceptedAnswer.text: Missouri requires applicants to be at least 21 years old to be eligible for a Licensed Dietitian credential.

mainEntity.name: Does Missouri have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Missouri. To work in Missouri, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Missouri?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Missouri while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
